assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states Regular channel clusters will be 'Auto-upgraded to the next available version in Regular channel' 2-4 weeks before EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response mentions Extended channel upgrades can be 'delayed up to several months' but doesn't specify the 24-month support window or that EoS timing is significantly different

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states 'No automatic upgrades' for No channel clusters and doesn't mention that both control plane and node pools are force-upgraded at EoS

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer EoS force-upgrades for 30 days

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed preparation timeline and immediate actions, emphasizing proactive planning over waiting for EoS

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Cloud Logging or EoS notifications as planning tools

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'kubectl deprecations' tool but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not use the term 'snowflaking' or specifically warn against manually freezing versions, though it does mention security risks for unsupported clusters
